text:
PET/CT SCAN WITH FDG -- WHOLE BODY

INDICATION:
Mr. Raymond Okafor is a 58-year-old male with known stage IV sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma (KRAS G12D-mutant, MSS) with bilobar hepatic metastases, status post laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy and multiple prior lines of systemic therapy. He was most recently maintained on 5-FU/leucovorin plus Avastin following discontinuation of Eloxatin due to cumulative peripheral neuropathy. Recent restaging CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis demonstrated mild interval enlargement of known hepatic metastatic foci, with the dominant segment VII lesion measuring 2.2 cm (up from 1.8 cm), and the emergence of three new bilateral pulmonary nodules (right lower lobe 8 mm, left upper lobe 6 mm, right middle lobe 5 mm) not previously identified on prior imaging. CEA has risen from a nadir of approximately 35.2 to 48.7 ng/mL. This study is requested to evaluate the metabolic activity and characterize the new pulmonary findings and to assess for additional sites of disease not captured on anatomic imaging alone.

TECHNIQUE:
Whole-body PET/CT was performed following intravenous injection of approximately 10.8 mCi of fluorine-18 fluorodeoxyglucose (18F-FDG). The patient fasted for at least 6 hours prior to injection. Serum glucose at time of injection was 108 mg/dL. Uptake time was 62 minutes. Attenuation-corrected PET images were acquired from the vertex of the skull to the mid-thigh and were co-registered with a low-dose CT scan obtained for attenuation correction and anatomic localization. A separate diagnostic-quality CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was obtained with intravenous contrast and reviewed in conjunction with PET data.

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:
No abnormal regions of FDG hypermetabolism in the brain parenchyma to suggest leptomeningeal or parenchymal metastatic involvement. The cerebral metabolic pattern is physiologically symmetric. No pathologically enlarged or metabolically active cervical, submandibular, or supraclavicular lymph nodes identified. No hypermetabolic lesions in the thyroid gland. Physiologic FDG activity noted in the salivary glands and tonsillar tissue bilaterally.

CHEST:
Lungs and Airways: Three pulmonary nodules are identified in the bilateral lungs and each demonstrates measurable FDG uptake above background.

-- Right lower lobe: A solid, well-defined nodule measuring 8 mm in greatest axial dimension is identified in the right lower lobe, posterior segment (segment 10), located approximately 1.4 cm from the visceral pleura. On co-registered PET, there is focal FDG uptake with an SUVmax of 3.4. This level of metabolic activity is above the background mediastinal blood pool (SUVmax approximately 1.8) and substantially above the lung parenchymal background. Given the known colorectal primary and the clinical context, this pattern of uptake is highly suspicious for a viable metastatic focus rather than a reactive or post-inflammatory process.

-- Left upper lobe: A solid nodule measuring 6 mm in the left upper lobe, posterior segment (segment 2-3 junction), shows focal FDG uptake with an SUVmax of 2.9. The degree of metabolic activity, while modest in absolute terms, is disproportionate relative to the small nodule size and is metabolically active by standard reference criteria. No central necrosis or cavitation on CT.

-- Right middle lobe: A third solid nodule measuring 5 mm is identified in the medial segment of the right middle lobe and demonstrates focal FDG uptake with an SUVmax of 2.1. While the absolute SUV is lower, the activity is above mediastinal blood pool and, in the context of the other two metabolically active nodules and the known metastatic colorectal cancer, is favored to represent a third site of pulmonary metastatic involvement.

No additional pulmonary nodules are identified beyond the three already described. No pleural effusions bilaterally. No FDG-avid pleural implants or pleural thickening. The central airways are patent without endobronchial lesions. No pneumonic consolidation.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is of normal contour. No FDG-avid mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. The largest right paratracheal lymph node measures approximately 8 mm in short axis and shows no meaningful FDG uptake. The aortopulmonary window contains a few small nodes, all metabolically quiescent. There are no hypermetabolic anterior mediastinal, subcarinal, or posterior mediastinal nodes.

Heart and Pericardium: Physiologic myocardial FDG uptake. No pericardial effusion. Physiologic mediastinal vascular activity.

Chest Wall and Osseous Thorax: A well-healed and metabolically quiescent subcutaneous right chest wall port is noted in the right infraclavicular region, with no surrounding FDG-avid inflammatory change or infection. The right subclavian vein catheter tract appears intact. No FDG-avid rib or sternal lesions to suggest osseous metastatic disease. No pathologic fractures or cortical destructive changes.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The liver is mildly enlarged with a smooth capsular contour. Four distinct foci of FDG hypermetabolism are identified within the hepatic parenchyma, corresponding to the previously characterized metastatic lesions:

-- Hepatic segment II lesion: A hypodense nodule measuring approximately 0.9 cm on the co-registered CT demonstrates FDG uptake with an SUVmax of 4.2. The lesion is situated along the inferior margin of the left lobe and is well-circumscribed. Metabolic activity is clearly above hepatic background parenchymal activity (estimated SUV approximately 2.5).

-- Hepatic segment IVa lesion: A hypodense focus measuring approximately 1.3 cm shows FDG uptake with an SUVmax of 5.8. This lesion is situated in the medial aspect of the left lobe superior to the falciform ligament insertion. There is no internal heterogeneity to suggest central necrosis on either CT or PET.

-- Hepatic segment VI lesion: A hypodense lesion measuring approximately 1.1 cm in the posterior right lobe shows FDG uptake with an SUVmax of 3.9. The spatial location is in the inferior right hepatic lobe, posteroinferiorly situated. Metabolically active, though with the lowest SUVmax among the four hepatic deposits.

-- Hepatic segment VII lesion (dominant): The most conspicuous hepatic focus is located in the posterosuperior right lobe, segment VII, adjacent to the right hepatic vein. This lesion measures approximately 2.2 cm on CT (interval increase from 1.8 cm on prior imaging) and is the most metabolically active hepatic focus, with an SUVmax of 6.1. There is mild central photopenia on PET possibly reflecting early central hypoxia or micro-necrosis, though the lesion periphery demonstrates robust circumferential FDG avidity. There is no frank satellite nodule, however close relationship to the right hepatic vein is again noted.

No FDG-avid liver lesions are identified beyond the four established sites described above. No new hepatic foci of suspicion. No ascites. No periportal or celiac axis FDG-avid lymphadenopathy.

Spleen: Normal size and metabolic activity. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Unremarkable morphology and FDG distribution. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilatation. Physiologic uniform parenchymal activity.

Adrenal Glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in size. No FDG-avid adrenal nodules. No adrenal metastatic foci.

Kidneys and Ureters: Normal bilateral renal size with symmetric physiologic FDG excretion into the collecting systems. No renal parenchymal lesions. No hydronephrosis. Normal ureteral drainage bilaterally.

Gastrointestinal Tract:
Gastric wall demonstrates physiologic and uniform FDG activity. Small bowel loops: no focally increased FDG uptake in the mesentery, bowel wall, or along the peritoneum. Colon: the sigmoid colon has been surgically resected; the colorectal anastomosis is identified in the left lower quadrant. There is no FDG-avid focus at the anastomotic site or adjacent pericolic tissue to suggest local recurrence. Physiologic mucosal activity in the residual colon and rectum. No peritoneal implants or omental caking are metabolically active.

Retroperitoneal and Mesenteric Lymph Nodes: No FDG-avid retroperitoneal, para-aortic, mesenteric, or iliac lymph nodes. A few scattered small mesenteric lymph nodes are noted by CT, all below 8 mm in short axis and without metabolic activity on PET, favored to represent post-surgical or reactive adenopathy.

Aorta and Vessels: Normal caliber aorta and iliac vessels. No FDG-avid periaortic tissue.

PELVIS:
Urinary Bladder: Physiologic FDG excretion in the urinary bladder. No bladder wall focal uptake.

Male Pelvic Organs: Prostate gland is of normal size without focal FDG hypermetabolism. No seminal vesicle abnormality.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No FDG-avid external or internal iliac, obturator, or inguinal lymph nodes.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:
Axial and Appendicular Skeleton: No FDG-avid osseous lesions identified throughout the imaged skeleton, including the vertebral column, pelvis, proximal femora, sternum, ribs, and calvarium. No evidence of lytic or blastic osseous metastases on the CT component. No pathologic compression deformities.

Soft Tissues and Musculature: No subcutaneous or muscular FDG-avid nodules. No soft tissue masses. Prior sigmoid colectomy scar demonstrates expected post-surgical changes without focal FDG uptake.

IMPRESSION:

1. HEPATIC METASTATIC DISEASE -- PROGRESSIVE: All four known hepatic metastatic foci remain FDG-avid and metabolically active. The dominant segment VII lesion demonstrates the highest metabolic activity (SUVmax 6.1) and has increased in size on the CT component. The segment II, IVa, and VI lesions also show persistent and robust FDG avidity. No new hepatic lesions are identified beyond the four previously established deposits. These findings are consistent with interval progression of hepatic metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma on maintenance therapy.

2. NEW BILATERAL PULMONARY METASTASES: The three bilateral pulmonary nodules identified on the recent CT (right lower lobe 8 mm, left upper lobe 6 mm, right middle lobe 5 mm) are all metabolically active with SUVmax values of 3.4, 2.9, and 2.1, respectively. In the clinical context of progressive metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma, FDG avidity in all three nodules is highly consistent with viable metastatic implants rather than benign or indeterminate etiologies. This represents new organ-site involvement (pulmonary), representing a change in disease stage from hepatic-only M1a to bilobar hepatic plus bilateral pulmonary metastases (M1b per AJCC staging criteria).

3. NO LOCOREGIONAL RECURRENCE: The colorectal anastomotic site demonstrates no FDG hypermetabolism and no CT evidence of anastomotic recurrence or locoregional nodal relapse. The peritoneal surfaces and omentum remain clear of FDG-avid disease.

4. NO ADDITIONAL METASTATIC SITES: No FDG-avid osseous lesions, adrenal lesions, peritoneal implants, mediastinal or hilar nodal disease, or other distant sites of metabolic activity are identified to suggest involvement beyond the hepatic and pulmonary compartments.

5. PORT DEVICE: Right chest wall subcutaneous implanted venous access device is intact and without surrounding FDG-avid inflammatory change or infection.
